reasoning: The most deterministic driver of diesel prices is the underlying price of crude oil. According to the October 2025 EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook, Brent crude oil prices are forecast to crash significantly from 2025 levels down to $52 per barrel in 2026. This huge fundamental driver strongly suggests that over the coming months, the diesel price will face massive downward pressure, more than offsetting any mild winter seasonal bumps. Recent FRED data confirms a sharp downward trend leading into late October (3.754 down steadily to 3.620 in just three weeks). Hence, prices are very likely to be lower across our resolution targets than the baseline on 2025-10-26. To accommodate standard statistical properties and literal prompt rules around forecasting, uncertainty increases for longer time horizons, drawing our probabilities closer toward 0.5 for the later dates, despite the extremely bearish structural picture.
